description: Fix bug where the plugin update notification (yellow dot + 'Update to vX.X.X' menu item) persists after a successful plugin update. Root cause: pruneStaleState in reload.ts only filters outdatedPlugins by whether the plugin still exists, not by whether the installed version now matches the latestVersion. Add comprehensive E2E tests covering the real update flow, reload interactions, and stress scenarios.

userStories.title: Prune outdatedPlugins entries when installed version matches latestVersion
userStories.description: Change pruneStaleState in reload.ts to filter out outdatedPlugins entries where the installed plugin version now matches or exceeds the latestVersion. Currently it only filters by whether the npm package name still exists in the registry.
acceptanceCriteria: After reload, an outdatedPlugins entry is removed when the registry plugin version equals latestVersion
After reload, an outdatedPlugins entry is retained when the registry plugin version is still older than latestVersion
After reload, an outdatedPlugins entry is removed when the plugin's npmPackageName no longer exists in the registry (existing behavior preserved)
Existing unit test 'filters outdatedPlugins to only include still-present npm packages' still passes
New unit test covers version-match pruning
New unit test covers version-still-outdated retention

userStories.title: E2E test: update notification clears after real update flow via pruneStaleState
userStories.description: Add E2E test that exercises the real update path: inject outdated data, trigger POST /reload (which calls pruneStaleState), and verify the update dot clears when the registry version matches latestVersion. This tests the actual pruneStaleState fix rather than the simulate-update test endpoint.
acceptanceCriteria: Test injects outdated data with latestVersion matching the plugin's actual installed version
After POST /reload, the update dot disappears because pruneStaleState removes the entry
The 'Update to vX.X.X' menu item is no longer visible after reload
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/side-panel-plugin-update.e2e.ts (add new test in this existing file)

Context:
The existing test file already has helpers: `setOutdatedPlugins` (line 51-67) injects fake outdated data via `POST /__test/set-outdated`, and `getPluginPackageInfo` (line 39-45) reads the plugin's current version.

The key insight: if we inject an outdated entry where `latestVersion` equals the plugin's actual installed version, then after a POST /reload (which triggers pruneStaleState), the entry should be pruned because version matches. This tests the US-001 fix end-to-end.

Test steps:
1. Setup: start server, extension, open tab, open side panel
2. Read the plugin's actual version (e.g., '0.0.83')
3. Inject outdated data: `{ name: pkgName, currentVersion: '0.0.82', latestVersion: '0.0.83', updateCommand: '...' }`
   - latestVersion intentionally equals the installed version
4. Wait for update dot to appear (the server just blindly stores what we injected)
5. Trigger POST /reload — this calls pruneStaleState which should now prune the entry
6. Wait for update dot to disappear
7. Open menu — verify no 'Update to v' menu item

The existing test patterns in this file show how to do all of this. Follow the same setup/teardown pattern.

Constraints:
- Add the new test as a new test.describe block in the existing file
- Use the same fixture helpers (setOutdatedPlugins, getPluginPackageInfo, etc.)
- The POST /reload trigger pattern is already used in the 'POST /reload preserves update dot' test (line 225-229)

userStories.title: E2E test: update notification persists after reload when plugin is still outdated
userStories.description: Add E2E test verifying that POST /reload does NOT clear the update notification when the plugin's installed version is still older than latestVersion.
acceptanceCriteria: Test injects outdated data with latestVersion higher than the plugin's actual version
After POST /reload, the update dot is still visible
The 'Update to vX.X.X' menu item is still present with the correct version
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/side-panel-plugin-update.e2e.ts (add new test)

Context:
This is the inverse of US-002 — verifying that the pruning doesn't over-aggressively remove entries that are genuinely outdated.

Test steps:
1. Setup: start server, extension, open tab, open side panel
2. Inject outdated data: `{ name: pkgName, currentVersion: actualVersion, latestVersion: '99.0.0', updateCommand: '...' }`
3. Wait for update dot to appear
4. Trigger POST /reload
5. Wait a reasonable amount (e.g., 5s) and verify update dot is STILL visible
6. Open menu — verify 'Update to v99.0.0' menu item is still present

Note: The existing test 'POST /reload preserves update dot when plugin has a pending update' (line 180) already tests this scenario. However, that test was written before the pruneStaleState fix — it passed because pruneStaleState wasn't version-aware (it always preserved). After US-001, we need to confirm it still passes with the version-aware pruning. If the existing test already covers this adequately, this story's scope can be reduced to just verifying the existing test passes (which the e2eCheckpoint on US-004 handles).

Constraints:
- If the existing test at line 180 already covers this scenario, add a comment noting that rather than duplicating it
- Only add a new test if the existing one is insufficient

userStories.title: E2E stress test: rapid reload spam does not corrupt update notification state
userStories.description: Add E2E stress test that fires multiple rapid POST /reload requests while an update notification is active, verifying the update dot settles to the correct state without flickering or corruption.
acceptanceCriteria: 5 rapid POST /reload requests are fired in sequence with 200ms gaps
After all reloads settle, the update dot reflects the correct state (visible if still outdated, hidden if version matches)
No duplicate plugin cards or visual corruption
Zero page errors
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/side-panel-plugin-update.e2e.ts (add new test)

Context:
The reload coalescing logic (POST /reload coalesces concurrent requests) means rapid reloads should be safe. But the interaction between reload coalescing and outdatedPlugins state needs stress testing.

Test steps:
1. Setup: start server, extension, open tab, open side panel
2. Inject outdated data with latestVersion = '99.0.0'
3. Wait for update dot to appear
4. Fire 5 sequential POST /reload requests with 200ms between each:
   ```ts
   for (let i = 0; i < 5; i++) {
     await fetch(`http://localhost:${port}/reload`, { method: 'POST', headers });
     await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 200));
   }
   ```
5. Wait 3s for everything to settle
6. Verify: update dot is still visible (plugin is still outdated)
7. Verify: exactly one plugin card for 'E2E Test' (no duplicates)
8. Check for zero page errors

The existing stress test pattern can be found in e2e/side-panel-reload.e2e.ts line 190 ('rapid POST /reload spam settles to correct state without duplicate cards').

Constraints:
- Use test.slow() since this involves multiple reloads
- The POST /reload coalescing means some requests may be batched — that's expected behavior

userStories.title: E2E test: simulate-update clears notification and version updates in UI
userStories.description: Add E2E test that uses the simulate-update endpoint and then verifies the version shown in the three-dot menu reflects the new version, confirming the full UI refresh after update.
acceptanceCriteria: Before update: menu shows current version (e.g., 'v0.0.83')
Before update: menu shows 'Update to v99.0.0'
After simulate-update: update dot is gone
After simulate-update: menu shows new version 'v99.0.0'
After simulate-update: no 'Update to v' menu item exists
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/side-panel-plugin-update.e2e.ts (add new test)

Context:
The existing test 'successful update changes version, clears update dot and menu item' (line 395) covers update dot clearance but doesn't verify the version text in the menu changes. The VersionItem component in PluginMenu.tsx shows `v{plugin.version}` in the menu.

This test strengthens the coverage by checking the version label updates in the UI, confirming the plugins.changed notification carries the updated version all the way to the rendered component.

Test steps:
1. Setup: start server, extension, open tab, open side panel
2. Note the current version from PluginMenu (open menu, read version text)
3. Inject outdated data with latestVersion = '99.0.0'
4. Wait for update dot
5. Open menu — verify version shows current and 'Update to v99.0.0' is present
6. Close menu
7. Call simulateUpdate(port, secret, 'e2e-test', '99.0.0')
8. Wait for update dot to disappear
9. Open menu — verify version now shows 'v99.0.0' and no Update menu item

The VersionItem renders the version string. Look at PluginMenu.tsx for the exact rendering — likely `v{plugin.version}` as a disabled menu item or label.

Constraints:
- Use the existing simulateUpdate helper from the file
- Match existing test patterns for menu interaction (menuButton.click, keyboard.press('Escape'))

userStories.title: E2E stress test: concurrent update + reload race condition
userStories.description: Add E2E stress test that fires a simulate-update and POST /reload concurrently to verify the update notification state is consistent regardless of which operation completes first.
acceptanceCriteria: Concurrent simulate-update and POST /reload do not cause duplicate plugin cards
After both settle, the update dot is gone (plugin is now at latest version)
Zero page errors
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/side-panel-plugin-update.e2e.ts (add new test)

Context:
This tests the race between:
- simulateUpdate: directly mutates registry version, clears outdated entry, sends plugins.changed
- POST /reload: re-runs discovery, calls pruneStaleState (which now checks versions), sends plugins.changed

Both paths send plugins.changed notifications. The side panel receives two rapid state updates. It should settle to the correct final state.

Test steps:
1. Setup: start server, extension, open tab, open side panel
2. Inject outdated data with latestVersion = '99.0.0'
3. Wait for update dot
4. Fire both concurrently:
   ```ts
   await Promise.all([
     simulateUpdate(port, secret, 'e2e-test', '99.0.0'),
     fetch(`http://localhost:${port}/reload`, { method: 'POST', headers }),
   ]);
   ```
5. Wait 3s for everything to settle
6. Verify: update dot is gone
7. Verify: one plugin card (no duplicates)
8. Verify: zero page errors

Constraints:
- This is the final story and has e2eCheckpoint: true — ralph runs the full E2E suite
- test.slow() due to multiple async operations
